name: Honey (food medium)

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Dominant monosaccharide; fructose > glucose is diagnostic of honey. ~227 mmol/100g (MW 180).

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Second dominant monosaccharide; substrate for glucose-oxidase-derived gluconic acid. ~198 mmol/100g.

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence: Reported free galactose in USDA honey panel.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Principal disaccharide; part of the higher-oligosaccharide fraction of honey.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Low sucrose is a ripeness/authenticity marker (invertase hydrolysis).

components.name: gluconic acid
components.source: Mato et al. 1998, J. Agric. Food Chem. doi:10.1021/jf970012c
components.evidence: PRINCIPAL organic acid of honey (64.6-99.8% of total organic acids); enzymatic total D-gluconic acid mean 7.37 g/kg (range 3.91-11.71 g/kg). Produced from glucose by bee glucose oxidase. Was MISSING from the FooDB profile.

components.name: formic acid
components.evidence: Mean ~30-98 mg/kg across varieties; minor relative to gluconic.

components.name: lactic acid
components.evidence: ~19-101 mg/kg; highest in buckwheat, absent in linden.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: ~15-29 mg/kg, present in all varieties.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: ~4-38 mg/kg; highest in buckwheat/rape.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: ~19 mg/kg, mainly honeydew honeys.

components.name: acetic acid
components.evidence: Consistently present minor volatile acid; not reliably quantified in cited panels.

components.name: pyroglutamic acid
components.evidence: Reported minor honey acid; unquantified here.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Trace, 0.09-0.42 mg/kg.

components.name: L-proline
components.source: USDA FDC 169640; Chen et al. 2024 PMC11011401 (China honeys)
components.evidence: DOMINANT free amino acid of honey (41.7-80.5% of total free AA); ~27-57 mg/100g across floral types, up to ~44 mg/100g buckwheat. Ripeness marker (>20 mg/100g in normal honey).

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Includes bee enzymes (invertase, glucose oxidase, diastase) and free amino acids; small carbon/nitrogen pool.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Second most abundant free AA after proline in most floral honeys; unquantified midpoint here.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid; present in the free-AA spectrum.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-alanine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-tyrosine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-histidine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-isoleucine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-threonine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: glycine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Minor free amino acid (L-serine is the biological form; current 'D-Serine' entry is a BiGG stereochemistry artifact).

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral of honey (~50% of ash).

components.name: phosphorus
components.evidence: Phosphate source.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Consolidates the duplicate FooDB iron entries (Iron / Iron Fe3+ / Fe2+ mitochondria) into one measured value.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Present counter-ion; not separately quantified in USDA panel.

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence: Trace sulfur source; unquantified.

components.name: thiamine (B1)
components.evidence: Trace / near zero in USDA honey panel.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Honey moisture ~17%; the low water activity is why honey is defined by very high sugar osmolarity.

remove.name: Lactose C12H22O11
remove.reason: Milk disaccharide; biologically implausible in honey (no lactose in nectar/honey). FooDB artifact — remove.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: BiGG compartmentalized-iron artifact, not a distinct honey nutrient; consolidated into the single measured iron value.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Duplicate iron entry (redox/compartment variant); consolidated into one iron value.

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: Atmospheric/dissolved gas, not a compositional honey nutrient; environmental boundary metabolite.

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: Atmospheric gas, not a honey nutrient; environmental boundary metabolite.

remove.name: Phytomenadione
remove.reason: Vitamin K1 is a plant-leaf phylloquinone; not reported in USDA honey panel and implausible at nutritive level in honey — likely FooDB predicted, drop.

remove.name: L-cystine
remove.reason: Not a characteristic honey free amino acid; sulfur-AA cystine is a likely FooDB prediction artifact, drop unless quantified.

remove.name: Glycine betaine
remove.reason: Not an established quantified honey nutrient; FooDB predicted phytochemical — drop from the growth-relevant profile.

missing_added: gluconic acid (0.74 g/100g — the DOMINANT organic acid of honey, previously absent)
specific sugar amounts (fructose 40.9, glucose 35.7, galactose 3.1, maltose 1.4, sucrose 0.9 g/100g)
minor organic acids with amounts (formic, lactic, quinic, malic, oxalic, citric, succinic, fumaric)
quantified L-proline (dominant free amino acid) + total protein
quantified vitamins and minerals from USDA panel
keep_uncovered: chrysin / pinocembrin / galangin (genuine honey flavonoids; real phytochemicals, no BiGG metabolite)
sources: Mato et al. 1998, Enzymatic Determination of Total D-Gluconic Acid in Honey, J. Agric. Food Chem. doi:10.1021/jf970012c
Jasicka-Misiak et al. 2024, Organic Acids in Monofloral and Honeydew Honeys from Poland, PMC12608658
Chen et al. 2024, Free Amino Acid Composition of Seven Honey Species in China, PMC11011401
Stinson et al. 1960, The composition of honey V: organic acids, Arch. Biochem. Biophys. doi:10.1016/0003-9861(60)90003-5
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are D-fructose (40.9 g/100g) and D-glucose (35.7 g/100g); the defining and previously-missing organic acid is gluconic acid (~0.74 g/100g, from bee glucose oxidase acting on glucose), the principal acid of honey. Free amino acids are dominated by proline. Very low water activity (~17% moisture) means honey is an extreme high-osmolarity sugar medium.
